sources: Benzinga (WIIM, ratings, earnings) + live universe prices + Jaleel Capital framework

macro.headline: Hawkish Fed, energy & geopolitics in the driver's seat
macro.body: Fed Chair Warsh scrapped forward guidance and the dot plot leans toward hikes, not cuts, so long-duration high-multiple growth (NOW, ADBE, NVDA, IOT) stays rate-sensitive. Energy and the Strait of Hormuz headlines are the live swing factor on inflation.

risk.detail: FY guide cut (Jun 4): Americas comps −5% (5th straight), tariffs gutted margins, China stalled.
FY26 growth cut 10.5→7–8.5%; Watchman/EP/urology soft; mgmt guides flat through Q3.
Beat & raised (Jun 11) yet sold off; AI-moat fear (Claude Design), CFO exit, ARR de-emphasis — the narrative, not the numbers, drives it.
China export-license tightening + Senate scrutiny; the memory/chip complex swings on AI-spending sentiment.
No bad news — strong Q1, IBM AI deal. But it's the biggest weight + a high multiple; in a hawkish-Fed software de-rate it leads the drawdown.
Trump MFN pricing deals (Gilead, Novartis signed) + import tariffs put a pricing-pressure overhang on the whole pharma cluster.
FY EPS guide cut on deal financing + a weak flu season; revenue raised. Defensive — a yellow flag, not a red one.

catalysts.why: Q-results for Coca-Cola — guidance is the catalyst.

As vessels transit the Strait of Hormuz under a UN evacuation program. Also, President Trump said he has ordered a DOJ investigation into retail fuel price gouging, which may lead to lower margins going forward.
Yields easing relieve long-duration growth; a hawkish surprise from Chair Warsh hits them. CPI/jobs prints set the tone.

sectors.driver: Micron Technology reported strong Q3 financial results
sectors.note: After Micron Technology reported strong Q3 financial results. Also, the industry may be rallying alongside Qualcomm after the company provided new guidance up to FY29.
sectors.src: Benzinga WIIM · flags a divergence vs our prices

sectors.driver: The House and Senate sent a bipartisan housing bill designed to lower ho…
sectors.note: After the House and Senate sent a bipartisan housing bill designed to lower housing costs for President Trump's signature. Sentiment was further boosted by KB Home’s better-than-expected second-quarter sales results.

sectors.driver: Vessels transit the Strait of Hormuz under a UN evacuation program
sectors.note: As vessels transit the Strait of Hormuz under a UN evacuation program. Also, President Trump said he has ordered a DOJ investigation into retail fuel price gouging, which may lead to lower margins going forward.

sectors.driver: Possible sympathy with Oracle after the company reported Q4 financial re…
sectors.note: Amid possible sympathy with Oracle after the company reported Q4 financial results and announced a $40 billion financing plan for FY27. The financing needs to fund aggressive capex spending may be giving investors pause.

sectors.driver: Oil prices and bond yields retreat amid optimism for a lasting U.S.-Iran…
sectors.note: As oil prices and bond yields retreat amid optimism for a lasting U.S.-Iran peace agreement, which may lead to cheaper borrowing for consumers.
